title: Floral Smoky Filipino Tofu Skewers
description: A light, vegetarian Filipino dish that marries floral rose water with smoky tandoor spices, perfectly suited for mealprep and served on skewers.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Press the tofu for at least 30 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into 2 cm cubes.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, cane vinegar, sugar, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, ground ginger, and rose water until the sugar dissolves.
Toss the tofu cubes in the melted shortening until evenly coated.
Add the tofu to the spice mixture and gently stir until each cube is well coated.
Thread the tofu cubes onto bamboo skewers (soak them in water for 30 minutes if using wooden skewers).
Arrange the skewers on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 18–20 minutes, turning halfway through, until the edges are golden and slightly charred.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle chopped mint over the skewers and serve warm or at room temperature.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days; reheat in a toaster oven or microwave.
